summary:
The provided evidence does not support either affirmative or negative claims about whether flexible instruments can achieve adequate femoral tunnel lengths at 90° of knee flexion in ACL reconstruction due to a complete lack of studies addressing this specific technical application. Therefore, based solely on available literature, the answer remains uncertain—best represented by “maybe.”  

\boxed{C}

The reviewed medical literature contains no studies examining how flexible instruments perform when forming femoral tunnels at 90° of knee flexion during ACL surgery. As a result, neither confirmation nor denial exists regarding their capacity to generate sufficient tunnel length—a critical factor affecting surgical outcomes—in this position.
